channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kevin Lerena to defend WBC bridgerweight title live on SABC and SuperSport in Belgium
item.description: Kevin 'Two Guns' Lerena is set to defend his WBC bridgerweight title against Ryad Merhy tomorrow night at the Hall des Expositions in Charleroi, Belgium. The fight will be broadcast live on SABC 2 at ...

item.title: Mother and son survive 10 days lost in the bush thanks to a life-saving note
item.description: In October 2017, Michelle Pittman and her nine-year-old son Dylan set out for what was meant to be a short day trip in Mount Royal National Park, NSW. Without food or water, they became disoriented in...

item.title: Senator Jinggoy Estrada faces new plunder and graft charges over flood control budget
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Senator Jinggoy Estrada is once again facing legal challenges as he is charged with plunder and graft related to alleged irregularities in flood control budget insertions. This m...

item.title: Vienna court convicts suspect over alleged plot targeting Taylor Swift concert
item.description: A court in Vienna has convicted a suspect in connection with an alleged plot targeting a Taylor Swift concert, according to a report by Deutsche Welle published on May 29, 2026. The case centres on se...

item.title: AI Skills Increase Demand and Salaries for Data Engineers in 2026
item.description: The article analyzes 6,736 active Data Engineer job postings from May 2026 to examine how artificial intelligence is changing the profession. While traditional responsibilities such as SQL, Python, cl...

item.title: Enhanced Games Sees Swimmer Set Unofficial World Record Amid Legalized PED Use
item.description: At the controversial Enhanced Games in Las Vegas, Greek swimmer Kristian Gkolomeev set a 50-meter freestyle time of 20.81 seconds, surpassing the official world record but in a context where performan...

item.title: UN includes Israeli and Russian forces on list for conflict-related sexual violence
item.description: According to a UN report reviewed by AFP, Israeli and Russian security forces have been officially added to the United Nations blacklist for engaging in sexual violence during conflicts. The annual li...

item.title: Report alleges Georgia Senate candidate falsely listed local officials as endorsers on campaign website
item.description: A report has accused Rep. Mike Collins (R-GA), a Republican candidate in Georgia’s Senate primary race, of falsely claiming endorsements from local officials on his campaign website. According to repo...

item.title: Cristine Reyes shares updates on wedding plans and timeline with fiancé Gio Tingson
item.description: Actress Cristine Reyes has recently opened up about her upcoming wedding plans with her fiancé, Gio Tingson, sharing insights into their possible timeline and preparations. In a report by ABS-CBN Ente...

item.title: Kenyan Youth Who Breached Security During Ruto Event Offered Employment Opportunity
item.description: A young man who caused a security scare after managing to reach and hug Kenyan President William Ruto during a public event has reportedly been offered a job, according to statements from the Office o...

item.title: Atiku and Amaechi meet in Abuja amid ongoing ADC presidential ticket dispute
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar on Thursday paid a visit to former Minister of Transportation, Rotimi Amaechi, at his Abuja residence, as tensions continue to rise over the African Democratic Con...

item.title: Starmer and Burnham Respond to Blair's Critique of Labour Policies
item.description: Tensions within the Labour Party surfaced on Thursday as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer and Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham openly rebuked former Prime Minister Sir Tony Blair's recent critique...

item.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Rocket Explodes During Engine Test at Cape Canaveral
item.description: On May 28, 2026, a Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during an engine-firing test at Launch Complex 36 in Cape Canaveral, Florida. The incident occurred at around 9 p.m., shaking nearby homes and ...

item.title: Governor presents policy address outlining governance and development priorities in Kerala Assembly session
item.description: The Kerala Legislative Assembly witnessed the Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar delivering the official policy address in Thiruvananthapuram during the ongoing session. The address, approved earlie...

item.title: Zanzibar steps up diplomatic talks to restore US and UK consular services and boost investment ties
item.description: Zanzibar government has continued to intensify diplomatic discussions with the United States and the United Kingdom aiming at convincing the two countries to reopen their consular offices on the islan...

item.title: New Zealand Budget 2026 continues road-focused transport spending despite rising fuel supply risks
item.description: New Zealand’s Budget 2026 has maintained a strong emphasis on road infrastructure despite growing global fuel supply risks linked to ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The budget alloca...

item.title: Israel freezes ties with UN after being added to sexual violence list alongside Hamas and ISIS
item.description: Israel has announced it is freezing relations with the office of UN Secretary-General António Guterres after being included on a UN report listing parties suspected of committing sexual violence in co...

item.title: URC play-offs present key opportunity for six Springbok contenders to impress
item.description: With the United Rugby Championship (URC) entering its knockout phase, attention turns to several South African players who are hoping to strengthen their claims for Springbok selection. The intensity ...

item.title: Kenya Lionesses player Michelle Alivitsa dies after battling illness
item.description: The Kenyan rugby community is in mourning following the death of Kenya Lionesses player Michelle Alivitsa, who passed away while undergoing treatment for an illness. According to reports from the Keny...

item.title: Study in mice suggests CBD may reduce brain inflammation linked to Alzheimer’s disease
item.description: A new study published in eNeuro suggests that cannabidiol (CBD), a compound derived from cannabis, may have the potential to reduce neuroinflammation associated with Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers f...
